verses.text: Then Moses and Israel sing: I will sing unto the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously; the horse and rider He has thrown into the sea. The LORD is my strength and my song; He has become my salvation. He is my God, and I will prepare Him a habitation. Guidance: The first song of redeemed Israel is not about themselves — it is about the LORD. After deliverance, the songs you sing teach you who you are.

Then Moses and Israel sing: I will sing unto the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously; the horse and rider He has thrown into the sea. The LORD is my strength and my song; He has become my salvation. He is my God, and I will prepare Him a habitation. Guidance: The first song of redeemed Israel is not about themselves — it is about the LORD. After deliverance, the songs you sing teach you who you are.

The LORD is a man of war; the LORD is His name. Pharaoh's chariots and host He has cast into the sea; his chosen captains are drowned in the Red Sea. The depths covered them; they sank to the bottom as a stone. Guidance: The song names what God did concretely. Worship that doesn't remember specifics tends to fade. Name the thing God did.

The LORD is a man of war; the LORD is His name. Pharaoh's chariots and host He has cast into the sea; his chosen captains are drowned in the Red Sea. The depths covered them; they sank to the bottom as a stone. Guidance: The song names what God did concretely. Worship that doesn't remember specifics tends to fade. Name the thing God did.

The LORD is a man of war; the LORD is His name. Pharaoh's chariots and host He has cast into the sea; his chosen captains are drowned in the Red Sea. The depths covered them; they sank to the bottom as a stone. Guidance: The song names what God did concretely. Worship that doesn't remember specifics tends to fade. Name the thing God did.

Thy right hand, O LORD, is glorious in power; Thy right hand has dashed the enemy in pieces. In greatness of excellency Thou hast overthrown them that rose against Thee. Thy wrath consumed them as stubble. With the blast of Thy nostrils the waters gathered. Guidance: Hebrew worship gives God body. His right hand, His breath — concrete words for an unseen power. Worship doesn't need abstract language to be true.

Thy right hand, O LORD, is glorious in power; Thy right hand has dashed the enemy in pieces. In greatness of excellency Thou hast overthrown them that rose against Thee. Thy wrath consumed them as stubble. With the blast of Thy nostrils the waters gathered. Guidance: Hebrew worship gives God body. His right hand, His breath — concrete words for an unseen power. Worship doesn't need abstract language to be true.

The enemy said: I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword. Thou didst blow with Thy wind; the sea covered them; they sank as lead in the mighty waters. Guidance: Five times Pharaoh said I will. One puff of God's breath silenced every one. Pride that lists its plans aloud often ends in the same breath.

The enemy said: I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword. Thou didst blow with Thy wind; the sea covered them; they sank as lead in the mighty waters. Guidance: Five times Pharaoh said I will. One puff of God's breath silenced every one. Pride that lists its plans aloud often ends in the same breath.

Who is like unto Thee, O LORD, among the gods? Who is like Thee — glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders? Thy right hand stretched out; the earth swallowed them. Thou in Thy mercy hast led forth the people Thou hast redeemed, guiding them by Thy strength to Thy holy habitation. Guidance: The song moves from victory to redemption. Salvation is not only escape from Egypt; it is being led toward God's presence.

Who is like unto Thee, O LORD, among the gods? Who is like Thee — glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders? Thy right hand stretched out; the earth swallowed them. Thou in Thy mercy hast led forth the people Thou hast redeemed, guiding them by Thy strength to Thy holy habitation. Guidance: The song moves from victory to redemption. Salvation is not only escape from Egypt; it is being led toward God's presence.

Who is like unto Thee, O LORD, among the gods? Who is like Thee — glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders? Thy right hand stretched out; the earth swallowed them. Thou in Thy mercy hast led forth the people Thou hast redeemed, guiding them by Thy strength to Thy holy habitation. Guidance: The song moves from victory to redemption. Salvation is not only escape from Egypt; it is being led toward God's presence.

The peoples shall hear and be afraid; sorrow shall seize Palestina's inhabitants. The dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab tremble; all the inhabitants of Canaan melt away. Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of Thine arm they shall be still as stone. Guidance: One miracle ripples across nations. What God does for His people becomes a sermon to the watching world.

The peoples shall hear and be afraid; sorrow shall seize Palestina's inhabitants. The dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab tremble; all the inhabitants of Canaan melt away. Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of Thine arm they shall be still as stone. Guidance: One miracle ripples across nations. What God does for His people becomes a sermon to the watching world.

What God does for His people becomes a sermon to the watching world.

Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of Thine inheritance — the place, O LORD, which Thou hast made for Thee to dwell in, the sanctuary which Thy hands have established. The LORD shall reign forever. Guidance: The song begins with the sea and ends with the throne. God's deliverance is never just an escape — it is on the way to a sanctuary where He reigns.

Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of Thine inheritance — the place, O LORD, which Thou hast made for Thee to dwell in, the sanctuary which Thy hands have established. The LORD shall reign forever. Guidance: The song begins with the sea and ends with the throne. God's deliverance is never just an escape — it is on the way to a sanctuary where He reigns.

For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and horsemen into the sea, and the LORD brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land in the midst of the sea. Guidance: The narration reasserts the contrast — same sea, two outcomes. Walking on dry ground in the middle of judgment is what salvation looks like.

Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron, took a timbrel; all the women went out after her with timbrels and dances. Miriam answered: sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously; the horse and rider He has thrown into the sea. Guidance: Miriam is named a prophetess here — the first woman in Scripture to bear that title. Women lead the response to deliverance with their bodies, their instruments, their voices.

Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron, took a timbrel; all the women went out after her with timbrels and dances. Miriam answered: sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously; the horse and rider He has thrown into the sea. Guidance: Miriam is named a prophetess here — the first woman in Scripture to bear that title. Women lead the response to deliverance with their bodies, their instruments, their voices.

Moses brings Israel from the Red Sea into the wilderness; three days they find no water. They come to Marah, but cannot drink — the waters are bitter. The people murmured against Moses: what shall we drink? Guidance: Three days after walking through the parted sea, they are complaining about water. Memory of God's mercy fades fast. Don't be shocked by the speed of your own forgetting.

He cried to the LORD, who showed him a tree; when cast into the waters, they were made sweet. There the LORD made a statute, and proved them: if you hearken to His voice and keep His commandments, I will put none of these diseases on you — for I am the LORD that healeth thee. Guidance: God reveals a healing name in the middle of bitterness. The name Jehovah-Rapha — the LORD who heals — is given over a bitter spring made sweet.

He cried to the LORD, who showed him a tree; when cast into the waters, they were made sweet. There the LORD made a statute, and proved them: if you hearken to His voice and keep His commandments, I will put none of these diseases on you — for I am the LORD that healeth thee. Guidance: God reveals a healing name in the middle of bitterness. The name Jehovah-Rapha — the LORD who heals — is given over a bitter spring made sweet.

And they came to Elim, where were twelve wells of water and threescore and ten palm trees; and they encamped there by the waters. Guidance: Twelve wells — one for each tribe. Seventy palms — one for each of the seventy who came down to Egypt with Jacob. God's provision after bitterness is not generic; it is exactly fitted to who you are.
